content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 4.5.1–4.5.3 WARN — push entitlement (DealAlerts.entitlements:6) paired with hourly repeating marketing pushes (NotificationScheduler.swift:12, `timeInterval: 3600, repeats: true`) and metadata promising "EVERY HOUR … unlimited notifications" (fastlane/metadata/en-US/description.txt:3) may trigger review questions.
Pierre: Apple treats notifications used purely for promotion or spam as an abuse of the push service under 4.5.3–4.5.4, and reviewers do read metadata for these promises. Here both the code (an hourly repeating "Hot deal alert!" notification) and the store description explicitly advertise unlimited hourly marketing pushes, which is a classic spam-push pattern. Rework notifications to be user-initiated (e.g. alerts for deals the user opted into), add frequency controls, and remove the "every hour / unlimited" language from the description.
